channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Doctors visiting a Gaza hospital are stunned by the war's toll on Palestinian children
item.description: DEIR AL-BALAH, Gaza (AP) — An international team of doctors visiting a hospital in central Gaza was prepared for the worst. But the gruesome impact Israel’s war against Hamas is having on Palestinian children still left them stunned.
item.dc:creator: WAFAA SHURAFA and KAREEM CHEHAYEB, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Central American and Mexican families mourn the workers lost in the Baltimore bridge collapse
item.description: The construction workers who went missing in the Baltimore bridge collapse came to the Maryland area from Mexico or Central America, including an enterprising Honduran father and husband who started a delivery business before the pandemic forced him to find other work, according to his family.
item.dc:creator: WFLA, NBC

item.title: Lego tells police to stop hiding suspects' faces with toy heads
item.description: Lego has since "respectfully asked" the department to "refrain from using their intellectual property in our social media content.
item.dc:creator: Tony Kurzweil, NewsNation

item.title: VP Harris says US agencies must show their AI tools aren't harming people's safety or rights
item.description: U.S. federal agencies must show that their artificial intelligence tools aren't harming the public, or stop using them, under new rules unveiled by the White House on Thursday.
item.dc:creator: MATT O'BRIEN, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Biden is announcing a new rule to protect consumers who purchase short-term health insurance plans
item.description: President Joe Biden on Thursday announced new steps to protect consumers who buy short-term health insurance plans that critics say amount to junk. A new rule finalized by the Democratic president's administration will limit these plans to just three months.
item.dc:creator: DARLENE SUPERVILLE, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Biden fundraiser in NYC with Obama, Clinton nets a whopping $25M, campaign says. It's a new record
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — A fundraiser for President Joe Biden on Thursday in New York City that also stars Barack Obama and Bill Clinton is raising a whopping $25 million — setting a new record for the most-ever haul for a single political event, his campaign said.
item.dc:creator: CHRIS MEGERIAN and COLLEEN LONG, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Could protective barriers have prevented Baltimore bridge collapse?
item.description: The Francis Scott Key Bridge in Baltimore did not appear to have any protective barriers in place before it was struck early Tuesday, raising serious questions.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Dave Portnoy raises 'about $240,000' for family of fallen NYPD officer Jonathan Diller
item.description: Barstool Sports founder Dave Portnoy said on social media that he raised over $200,000 for the family of New York Police Department officer Jonathan Diller, who was killed Monday night.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: It's a bittersweet Easter for chocolate lovers and African cocoa farmers but big brands see profits
item.description: ACCRA, Ghana (AP) — Shoppers may get a bitter surprise in their Easter baskets this year. Chocolate eggs and bunnies are more expensive than ever as changing climate patterns eat into global cocoa supplies and the earnings of farmers in West Africa.
item.dc:creator: FRANCIS KOKUTSE and JESSSICA DONATI, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Talks resume on bringing Israeli officials to the US to discuss Gaza operation, the White House says
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Talks have restarted aimed at bringing top Israeli officials to Washington to discuss potential military operations in Gaza, after Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu canceled a planned visit this week because he was angry about the U.S. vote on a U.N.
item.dc:creator: COLLEEN LONG and SEUNG MIN KIM, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Central American and Mexican families mourn the workers lost in the Baltimore bridge collapse
item.description: AZACUALPA, Honduras (AP) — The construction workers who went missing in the Baltimore bridge collapse came to the Maryland area from Mexico or Central America, including an enterprising Honduran father and husband who started a delivery business before the pandemic forced him to find other work, acc
item.dc:creator: CLAUDIO ESCALÓN, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: NYC serial puncher: Victim says she recognizes attacker
item.description: Several women have taken to TikTok to detail they've allegedly been sucker-punched in the face by an unknown man in New York City.
item.dc:creator: Liz Jassin, NewsNation

item.title: Venezuelans are increasingly stuck in Mexico, explaining drop in illegal crossings to US
item.description: MEXICO CITY (AP) — Venezuelan migrants often have a quick answer when asked to name the most difficult stretch of their eight-country journey to the U.S. border, and it's not the dayslong jungle trek through Colombia and Panama with its venomous vipers, giant spiders and scorpions. It's Mexico.
item.dc:creator: CHRISTOPHER SHERMAN, ELLIOT SPAGAT and VALERIE GONZALEZ, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Dethroned crypto king Sam Bankman-Fried to be sentenced for defrauding FTX investors
item.description: Former crypto mogul Sam Bankman-Fried faces the potential of decades in prison when he is sentenced for his role in the 2022 collapse of FTX.
item.dc:creator: Ken Sweet And Larry Neumeister, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: What to know about the cargo ship Dali, a mid-sized ocean monster that took down a Baltimore bridge
item.description: Here's what to know about the cargo ship Dali that crashed into Baltimore's Francis Scott Key Bridge, causing it to collapse and leaving six bridge construction workers presumed dead.
item.dc:creator: NICK PERRY, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Powerball jackpot increases to $935 million after no one wins the top prize
item.description: DES MOINES, Iowa (AP) — The Powerball jackpot increased to an estimated $935 million after no one matched the six numbers drawn Wednesday night. The numbers selected were: 37, 46, 57, 60, 66 and the Powerball 8.
item.dc:creator: AP, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Hidden camera found in staff bathroom at children's hospital in Rhode Island: police
item.description: The employee accused of placing the camera in the bathroom has since been fired, according to a hospital spokesperson.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Doiron, KRON4

item.title: Army Corps of Engineers to deploy 1,100 personnel to Baltimore after bridge collapse
item.description: The crumbling bridge sent multiple cars and construction workers who had been filling potholes plunging into the chilly waters below.
item.dc:creator: Ellen Mitchell, KRON4

item.title: TIMELINE: NTSB details moments before cargo ship crashed into Baltimore bridge
item.description: The NTSB received six hours of voyage data recorder (VDR) data spanning from midnight to 6 a.m. on the day of the crash.
item.dc:creator: Kevin Accettulla, KRON4

item.title: Hunter Biden's lawyers push California judge to toss out tax charges
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — Attorneys for Hunter Biden asked a judge Wednesday to toss out the tax case accusing him of a four-year scheme to avoid paying $1.4 million in taxes while living an extravagant lifestyle.
item.dc:creator: STEFANIE DAZIO and LINDSAY WHITEHURST, Associated Press, KTLA 5

item.title: An indicted Tennessee judge is sent to jail after her bond is revoked for positive cocaine tests
item.description: MEMPHIS, Tenn. (AP) — A Tennessee criminal court judge was sent to jail Wednesday after her bond was revoked for testing positive for cocaine while she was out of custody pending a trial on charges of coercion of a witness and harassment.
item.dc:creator: AP, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Disney concept art, rare memorabilia available in upcoming auction
item.description: The collection will spotlight work from Marc and Alice Davis, former Disney Imagineers who worked on classic Disney movies and attractions.
item.dc:creator: Iman Palm, KTLA 5

item.title: Baltimore bridge workers' presumed deaths highlight dangers of essential jobs
item.description: The presumed deaths of six road workers on the Francis Scott Key Bridge in Baltimore are shining a spotlight on the risks associated with essential jobs, especially for low-income and immigrant workers.
item.dc:creator: Rafael Bernal, KTLA 5

item.title: Eclipse 2024: Will it affect your plants?
item.description: Researchers in the past have observed how animals react to a total solar eclipse, but how would it affect plants?
item.dc:creator: Rhea Caoile, Fox 5

item.title: State Department offers $10 million reward for info on UnitedHealthcare hackers
item.description: The State Department announced Wednesday it would provide a $10 million reward for information about the Blackcat ransomware group that hacked UnitedHealth Group in February.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, KTLA 5

item.title: Baltimore bridge collapse: Ships carrying cars and heavy equipment need to find a new harbor
item.description: Manufacturers and shippers are scrambling to figure out where they can load or unload cargo while the main operations of the Port of Baltimore remain shut down due to Tuesday's deadly collapse of the Francis Scott Key Bridge.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Diddy's alleged drug 'mule' arrested at Florida airport amid raids at rapper's homes
item.description: A known associate of Sean "Diddy" Combs and former Syracuse basketball player was arrested at a Florida airport Monday as federal agents raided the music mogul's Miami and Los Angeles mansions.
item.dc:creator: Kaycee Sloan, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Lego tells California police to stop hiding suspects' faces with toy heads
item.description: A California police department has agreed to stop using Lego toy heads to digitally block suspects' faces in social media posts after the Danish toy company stepped in.
item.dc:creator: Tony Kurzweil, Fox 5

item.title: Baltimore bridge collapse: Who are the 6 victims?
item.description: When a cargo ship smashed into the Francis Scott Key Bridge in Baltimore, a group of construction workers were on the span. The search for the missing turned to a "recovery" mission Wednesday, then a "salvage" operation, as officials said two bodies were recovered and four others were presumed dead.
item.dc:creator: Alix Martichoux, Fox 5

item.title: Most Americans support cannabis legalization for medical, recreational use: Pew
item.description: Story at a glance Most Americans — 88 percent — think cannabis should be legal for medical or recreational use, according to a recently published Pew Research Center survey.
item.dc:creator: Alejandra O'Connell-Domenech, The Hill

item.title: 8-year-old girl found dead in pipe after swimming at Texas hotel
item.description: Police said the girl was found “inside a large pipe in the pool area” and was pronounced dead by paramedics.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Must-see video: Thousands upon thousands of rays gather in Florida waters
item.description: Aerial footage captured the jaw-dropping moment thousands upon thousands of cownose rays crowded Tampa Bay waters last month.
item.dc:creator: Kaycee Sloan, The Hill

item.title: Disney, DeSantis-backed board reach settlement agreement
item.description: Disney and the state of Florida have reached a settlement agreement in their lawsuit about who controls Walt Disney World’s governing district.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, WGN 9

item.title: Retired grandmother still owes $108,000 in student debt 40 years after taking out loan
item.description: According to the U.S. Department of Education, 43 million Americans owe a collective $1.3 trillion in student loan debt.
item.dc:creator: Mike Lowe, The Hill

item.title: Cargo ship had engine maintenance in port before it collided with Baltimore bridge, officials say
item.description: The U.S. Coast Guard says the cargo ship that crashed into and collapsed a bridge in Baltimore underwent “routine engine maintenance” in port beforehand.
item.dc:creator: Lea Skene, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Hardy souls across New England and New York dig out after major winter storm
item.description: BOSTON (AP) — Thousands of hardy souls across New England spent Sunday digging out after a major weekend storm dumped more than two feet of snow in some areas, caused multiple road accidents, downed power lines and left hundreds of thousands across the Northeast in the dark, some perhaps for days.
item.dc:creator: STEVE LeBLANC, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: 85 years after a racist mob drove Opal Lee's family away, she's getting a new home on the same spot
item.description: FORT WORTH, Texas (AP) — When Opal Lee was 12, a racist mob drove her family out of their Texas home. Now, the 97-year-old community activist is getting closer to moving into a brand new home on the very same tree-lined corner lot in Fort Worth.
item.dc:creator: JAMIE STENGLE, Associated Press, WGN 9
